AS a means of increasing the
fund toward the erection of the
Woman's club building the men,
bers of the organization will give
a dancing party early in February
for all persons who took part in the
musical extravaganza in 1908 and
those who took part in the perform
ance of Mother Goose For the
same purpose the club members
will entertain at a card party to-
day at the chamber of commerce.
Progressive five hundred and
bridge will furnish the entertain
ment and prizes will be awarded
In connection with these two af-
fairs book reception will be held
at Sanders hall Wednesday, by the
Woman's club for the benefit Of the
public library.


is SPOKANE GUEST.


Mrs. Minnie Grubbe, 1924 Sharpe
avenue. has as her guest Mrs.
Harry n. May of Wenatchee, Wash.
Mrs. May will remain in the city
until after the marriage of her
daughter, Grace, which takes place
early in February.


4 4


WHIST PARTY THURSDAY.
Mrs. J. r. Reinhardt has issued
invitations for an afternoon at
home next Thursday. The affair
will be informal and the guests
will be entertained at whist.


4 = =


SERIES OF INFORMAL TEAS


AT DOW HOME.


Mrs. j. X. Dow will be hostess on
Thursday and Friday afternoons of
this week, when she will entertain
at two informal teas at her home on
Tenth avenue. Eighty guests have
been hidden for both affairs.


4 4 =


VALENTINE SOCIAL.


The members of the Epworth
league of the First Methodist
church will entertain at Valen-
tine social on the evening of Febru-
AIRY at the church parlors on the
corner of Howard street and Third
avenue. A large crowd is expected
and the committee in charge is
making plans for a jolly good
time.

party Saturday evening at the Iio-
